title: CommonDefinitions
description: Reusable framework-neutral definitions shared across schemas-v2. Referenced by $ref from other schemas.

frameworkDescriptor.description: Identifies a UI framework as a PARAMETER, never an assumption. Any framework (legacy source or migration target) is described this way.

id.description: Framework identifier. Open vocabulary; these are examples, not an exhaustive enum.

version.description: Detected version or range, e.g. '1.8.3', '17.x', '18.2.0'. 'unknown' is forbidden past the intake phase.

evidenceRefBase.description: Content-addressed pointer to an artifact. Workspace-relative path + SHA-256; the harness verifies hashes at gate time. UNSEALED base: compose via allOf and add your own properties + `unevaluatedProperties:false` to extend it (see evidence-bundle items). For a standalone, closed pointer use `evidenceRef`.

path.description: Workspace-relative path; '..' and absolute paths are rejected.

evidenceRef.description: Sealed content-addressed pointer: `evidenceRefBase` with no additional properties. Use in slots that do not extend the shape.

unitId.description: Neutral unit id: unit:<kind>:<canonical-name>. `kind` is drawn from the neutral unit-kind vocabulary; the canonical name is framework-agnostic.
